summary: This is an early blog post from Dan Shapiro's site featuring a guest review by Brian Todoroff of the Bosch RA1165 undertable router base. The review praises the product as an affordable alternative to expensive router lifts, allowing above-table height adjustment for under $60. The reviewer notes minor issues with locating the drill hole for the adjustment screw and occasional router tilting when the latch is released. Overall, the product is recommended as a great value for hobbyist woodworkers who don't need a full professional router lift.
key_points: The Bosch RA1165 solves the problem of having to reach under a router table for height adjustments, offering above-table wrench access at under $60
Before this product, woodworkers had to either spend hundreds on a router lift or awkwardly adjust their router from underneath the table
Minor design issues include the lack of a drill hole template and occasional router tilting when the latch is released
The product is ideal for hobbyist woodworkers but professionals would still benefit from a dedicated router lift
money_quotes: It seems that sticking a router under a hole in a table and using it from the top should be a simple bit of engineering, but until recently it eluded most router manufactures.
Every time I looked at the fixed base I just knew that it should be a simple modification to find a longer screw for the adjustment and then drill one hole.
My wife will happily tell you that I come up with far more projects than I could possible ever get done.
Luckily someone from Bosch was hiding in the rafters and took my grand idea back to the factory.
If I were running a full time shop routing day in and day out I'd still by a nice router lift, but for something that gets used once a week it's perfect.
key_insight: A guest woodworking product review from Dan Shapiro's early blogging days, showcasing his longstanding connection to maker culture and hands-on engineering.
